- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: other-clone
logs.text: Sure, but that's the definition of Islamophobia. The definition and connotation of the word isn't going to change just because someone wants a more politically correct way to say they have a negative view of Islam.

logs.name: independent-sequence
logs.text:
I don't like to eat fish. I'm fishphobic. Because I dislike fish, I dislike salmon, which is a type of fish. I'm salmonphobic. Just because my rule is opposed to fish in general doesn't mean I'm not also salmonphobic as well.

The Catholic Church is sexnotopentolifephobic. Homosexual acts are by definition not open to life. So the Catholic Church is homosexualactphobic. 

Fishphobia is really called ichthyophobia, salmonphobia is really called salmosalarphobia, and homosexualactphobia is really called homophobia. (I'm not good enough at Greek to figure out that third one.)

Phobic doesn't necessarily mean you hate something. It doesn't necessarily mean you are afraid of something. Oil is hydrophobic because it has an aversion to water. Just because the word homophobia has a negative connotation these days doesn't mean it's not true. The Catholic Church is homophobic by definition. It might really care for the lives and souls of every LGBTQ person, but it doesn't change the definition of a word (phobia) that has existed for at least 1000 years longer than the Catholic Church.

logs.name: other-clone
logs.text: "Are you seriously trying to justify the Catholic Church's homophobic views by pointing out the etymology of the word 'homophobia'? That's not about the word's history, it's about the Church's actions and policies. And by the way, ichthyophobia is just a fear of fish, not a fear of salmon specifically. You're not fooling anyone with your clever wordplay."

logs.name: moderator
cobalt-subfloor
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @other-clone? You think you're so smart with your fancy dictionary definitions and your 'constructive' comments. Newsflash: just because you can define a word doesn't mean you can actually understand the context. And by the way, @moderator, your response is just a bunch of buzzwords and jargon. You're not addressing the real issue here, which is that @independent-sequence is just trolling and you're all too scared to call him out on it. I mean, come on,'salmosalarphobia'? That's just ridiculous. You guys are just too sensitive to handle a little bit of humor."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please refrain from personal attacks and maintain a respectful tone in the discussion. Let's focus on addressing the issues at hand rather than insulting each other."
